source.cookbook: Black Rican Vegan   Fire Plant Based Recipes from
source.author: Various

ingredients.item:
COMPRA LIST
2 (14-oz [400-g]) cans hearts of palm, drained and rinsed
2 tbsp (10 g) nori furikake, divided
1 (2-oz [55-g]) can jackfruit in brine, drained
2 tbsp (30 ml) olive oil
3 tbsp (45 ml) Sofrito
5 cloves garlic, minced
1 yellow onion, diced
1 cup (150 g) diced bell peppers, assorted colors
1 (8-oz [225-g]) can tomato sauce
4 cups (946 ml) vegetable stock
½ cup (45 g) olives with pimientos and capers
1½ tsp (1 [4-g] packet) Sazón or my homemade Sazón
1 tsp Adobo
1 tbsp (4 g) fresh oregano
Pinch of sugar (optional)
2 tbsp (2 g) chopped fresh cilantro
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.original:
COMPRA LIST
2 (14-oz [400-g]) cans hearts of palm, drained and rinsed
2 tbsp (10 g) nori furikake, divided
1 (2-oz [55-g]) can jackfruit in brine, drained
2 tbsp (30 ml) olive oil
3 tbsp (45 ml) Sofrito
5 cloves garlic, minced
1 yellow onion, diced
1 cup (150 g) diced bell peppers, assorted colors
1 (8-oz [225-g]) can tomato sauce
4 cups (946 ml) vegetable stock
½ cup (45 g) olives with pimientos and capers
1½ tsp (1 [4-g] packet) Sazón or my homemade Sazón
1 tsp Adobo
1 tbsp (4 g) fresh oregano
Pinch of sugar (optional)
2 tbsp (2 g) chopped fresh cilantro
Salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.item: Chop your hearts of palm into small pieces, place in a bowl and marinate it in 1 tablespoon (5 g) of the nori furikake.
ingredients.original: Chop your hearts of palm into small pieces, place in a bowl and marinate it in 1 tablespoon (5 g) of the nori furikake.

ingredients.item: Bring a medium-sized pot of water to a boil. Add the drained jackfruit to the boiling water and cook for 30 minutes to extract the brine. Remove from the water and pat out the excess liquid with a paper towel. Shred the jackfruit, season it in the remaining tablespoon (5 g) of nori furikake and set it aside.
ingredients.original: Bring a medium-sized pot of water to a boil. Add the drained jackfruit to the boiling water and cook for 30 minutes to extract the brine. Remove from the water and pat out the excess liquid with a paper towel. Shred the jackfruit, season it in the remaining tablespoon (5 g) of nori furikake and set it aside.
